root_text.an8.24:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā āḷaviyaṁ viharati aggāḷave cetiye.
root_text.an8.24:1.2: Atha kho hatthako āḷavako pañcamattehi upāsakasatehi parivuto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an8.24:1.4: Kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, hatthaka, imaṁ mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgaṇhāsī”ti?
root_text.an8.24:1.5: “Yānimāni, bhante, bhagavatā desitāni cattāri saṅgahavatthūni, tehāhaṁ imaṁ mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgaṇhāmi.
root_text.an8.24:1.14: Saṁvijjanti kho pana me, bhante, kule bhogā.
root_text.an8.24:1.15: Daliddassa kho no tathā sotabbaṁ maññantī”ti.
root_text.an8.24:1.17: Yoni kho tyāyaṁ, hatthaka, mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgahetuṁ.
root_text.an8.24:1.18: Ye hi keci, hatthaka, atītamaddhānaṁ mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgahesuṁ, sabbe te imeheva catūhi saṅgahavatthūhi mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgahesuṁ.
root_text.an8.24:1.19: Yepi hi keci, hatthaka, anāgatamaddhānaṁ mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgaṇhissanti, sabbe te imeheva catūhi saṅgahavatthūhi mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgaṇhissanti.
root_text.an8.24:1.20: Yepi hi keci, hatthaka, etarahi mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgaṇhanti, sabbe te imeheva catūhi saṅgahavatthūhi mahatiṁ parisaṁ saṅgaṇhantī”ti.
root_text.an8.24:2.1: Atha kho hatthako āḷavako bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.an8.24:2.2: Atha kho bhagavā acirapakkante hatthake āḷavake bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an8.24:2.3: “aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgataṁ hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ dhāretha.
root_text.an8.24:2.13: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgataṁ hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ dhārethā”ti.

translation_text.an8.24:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Āḷavī, at Āḷavī’s premier shrine.
translation_text.an8.24:1.2: Then the householder Hatthaka of Āḷavī, escorted by around five hundred lay followers, went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to Hatthaka:
translation_text.an8.24:1.4: How do you bring together such a large congregation?”
translation_text.an8.24:1.5: “Sir, I bring together such a large congregation by using the four ways of being inclusive as taught by the Buddha.
translation_text.an8.24:1.7: can be included by a gift, I include them by giving a gift.
translation_text.an8.24:1.9: can be included by kindly words, I include them by kindly words.
translation_text.an8.24:1.11: can be included by taking care of them, I include them by caring for them.
translation_text.an8.24:1.13: can be included by equality, I include them by treating them equally.
translation_text.an8.24:1.14: But also, worthy sir, my family is wealthy.
translation_text.an8.24:1.15: They wouldn’t think that a poor person was worth listening to in the same way.”
translation_text.an8.24:1.17: This is the right way to bring together a large congregation.
translation_text.an8.24:1.18: Whether in the past, future, or present, all those who have brought together a large congregation have done so by using these four ways of being inclusive.”
translation_text.an8.24:2.1: Then the Buddha educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired Hatthaka of Āḷavī with a Dhamma talk, after which he got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha before leaving.
translation_text.an8.24:2.2: Then, not long after Hatthaka had left, the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an8.24:2.3: “Mendicants, you should remember the householder Hatthaka of Āḷavī as someone who has eight amazing and incredible qualities.
translation_text.an8.24:2.13: You should remember the householder Hatthaka of Āḷavī as someone who possesses these eight amazing and incredible qualities.”
